title: as_svg default attributes xmlns:xlink + xmlns:ev
author: mcauser
body:
Looking to optimise the generated svg xml.
https://github.com/whomwah/rqrcode/blob/main/lib/rqrcode/export/svg.rb#L190-L191

`DEFAULT_SVG_ATTRIBUTES` includes:
* `xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"`
* `xmlns:ev="http://www.w3.org/2001/xml-events"`

If the generated xml only contains a `<rect>` and a `<path>`, can we remove these attributes?

`xmlns:xlink` was for `<use xlink:href="#id">`. Modern browsers now support using href directly `<use href="#id">`.
`xmlns:ev` was for event listeners, `<ev:listener event="click" ...>`, abandoned in favour of JS listeners.

Neither appear to be used and if removed reduces the generated svg by 88 chars.

title: Unable to generate a QR code without a border using Google strategy
author: kjvarga
body: I'm not able to generate a QR code without a border around it when using the Google strategy. When displaying the QR code on a white background the border is not needed, so I'd like to be able to render with no visible border. I've set `border_modules: 0` in my options but it doesn't completely remove the border. Example: ``` qr_code = RQRCode::QRCode.new('http://example.com', :m) png_data = qr_code.as_png( border_modules: 0, size: 128, ) ``` Generates the following: If I use the non-Google strategy as follows I can do it: ``` qr_code = RQRCode::QRCode.new('http://example.com', :m) png_data = qr_code.as_png( border_modules: 0, module_px_size: 1, resize_exactly_to: 128, ) ``` Generates the following: I think it's a by-product of how the Google strategy works, but seems inconsistent with the options as stated. Would be nice if it could omit the border and scale the image as needed.

title: [Suggestion]: Add ability to customize offset value for translate attribute for both coordinates.
author: vitaliiorlov
body:
Hello!
In my project, I need to use different values of offset to embed the generated non-standalone QR code into another SVG file.
I need to use Nokogiri to customize this value in generated io.

If it is possible, could you please add ability to set offset for x and y?


`lib/rqrcode/export/svg.rb:82`
```ruby
@result << %{<path d="#{path.join}" fill="#{color}" transform="translate(#{offset},#{offset}) scale(#{module_size})"/>}
```

Thank you!

title: Request for New Release on RubyGems
author: navidemad
body:
I hope you are doing well. Thank you for all the hard work you’ve put into maintaining this gem.

I noticed that there have been several commits merged into the main branch.
However, these changes haven’t been published as a new release on RubyGems yet.

Would it be possible to make a new release so that we can take advantage of the latest improvements via RubyGems?
It would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for considering this

title: Alternative faster SVG path implementation
author: severinraez
body:
Hi,

Would you be willing to accept an alternative implementation for the path based SVG exports?

We're running into a bottleneck in batch jobs where we generate several QR codes for Swiss payment slips and it takes > 1.5 seconds to generate one code. One option we might choose is to contribute a change to the gem.

The idea is as follows: Instead of trying to solve for a small set of connected SVG paths and then generating these, did you already think about generating one rectangle per "dot" using `M <x> <y> h 1 v 1 h -1 Z`? That would require a simple O(n) loop over the "dots" of the QR code. Maybe you've already tried it and it does not play well with the upscaling transform because the resulting rectangles don't neatly connect.

I'd implement this in two commits, first extracting the SVG test data into files and then implementing the change. That way you can visually verify in the PR that the generated results don't change, even if the internal structure of the paths changes.

Curious to get your opinion.

title: Png VCard qrcode return without any data
author: Tioneb12
body:
I'm working with an old rails app with rqrcode (version (0.10.1).

```
  def qrcode
    qrcode_data = "BEGIN:VCARD
      VERSION:4.0
      N:#{@participant.nom};#{@participant.prenom}
      ORG:#{@participant.entreprise}
      EMAIL:#{@participant.email}
      TEL;type=PREF:#{@participant.tel}
      END:VCARD"

    qrcode = RQRCode::QRCode.new(qrcode_data)

    png = qrcode.as_png(size: 200)

    IO.binwrite("tmp/#{@participant.id}-qrcode.png", png)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.png { send_data(png, type: 'image/png', disposition: 'inline') }
    end
  end
```

I get a png with a qrcode but without any datas.
![29708-qrcode](https://github.com/whomwah/rqrcode/assets/33897917/9d7653fa-09e8-4bdf-ab4a-08c0361f1024)

qrcode_data look good and qrcode return :

xxxxxxx x x       x x x  x    x        x xx xxx xxx  x x  xxxxxxx
x     x xx  x x x  xxx  x  xx  xx x xxx  x x x x  x  x  x x     x
x xxx x   x xxxxxxx xxx   x   x xxx    xx  xx  xx x  xx x x xxx x
x xxx x x   xx x x   xxx x  xx xxxxx xx   xxxxx      xxx  x xxx x
x xxx x x xx x   xxx  xx   x  xxxxx    x x      x x  x  x x xxx x
x     x xx  xxxxxxx   x x   x x   xxxx xx xxxxx    x xx   x     x
xxxxxxx x x x x x x x x x x x x x x x x x x x x x x x x x xxxxxxx

.....

as_svg return the same empty qrcode

I tried to upgrade to a never version (2.2.0) but have the same return.

What's wrong ?

I'm using :
ruby 2.6.6
rails 5.2.2

```rb

# Responds with:

# Responds with:

  { data: 'bar1', mode: :alphanumeric }
])

# Responds with:

title: Rails HTML safe strings
author: samrjenkins
body:
Thank you so much for your work on this project.

I am getting in touch because the HTML/XML strings returned by the renderers present an issue when it comes to HTML safety.

Currently we solve this in our project by calling `#html_safe` on the strings returned by the renderers. However, calling `#html_safe` irks me slightly and I would prefer to avoid it.

Is there a possible solution you could implement in this gem? I would understand if you didn't wish to implement Rails-specific functionality though.

Would you have any other suggestions (possibly from how you use this gem in your own Rails projects) for elegantly handling HTML safety?

I have attempted to extract the SVG path data from the SVG string produced by `as_svg` and to then construct an HTML safe string using Rails tag helpers. This quickly became quite messy!

I would be interested to hear your thoughts.

title: undefined method `dark?' for #<RQRCode::QRCode:0x00005649dee622a0
author: rijotech
body:
Hi, I'm facing a problem trying to generate a Visual QR code I'm getting this error message.
I'm using [this Github](https://github.com/orzFly/VisualQR)to generate the Visual QR Code. 

`undefined method `dark?' for #<RQRCode::QRCode:0x00005649dee622a0 @qrcode=QRCodeCore: @data='#<RQRCodeCore::QRSegment:0x00005649dee61f58>', @error_correct_level=2, @version=4, @module_count=33>`

I noticed this is the part generating the error.
`data[y + 1][x + 1] = true if qr.dark?(x, y)`

From here.
```
	def self.create(string, bgimg, options = {})
		options[:size] ||= 4
		options[:level] ||= :h
		options[:modified] ||= (options[:level] == :h ? 0.03 : 0)
		options[:bgcolor] ||= ChunkyPNG::Color(255, 255, 255)
		options[:dotpadding] ||= 0.25
		size = size(options[:size])
		qr = RQRCode::QRCode.new( string, :size => options[:size], :level => options[:level] )
		bw = (1.0 * bgimg.width / size).ceil
		bh = (1.0 * bgimg.height / size).ceil
		qrcode = ChunkyPNG::Image.new(bw * size, bh * size, ChunkyPNG::Color::TRANSPARENT)
		bb = bgimg.resample_bilinear(qrcode.width, qrcode.height)
		data = size.times.map { size.times.map { false } }
		qr.modules.each_index do |x|
			qr.modules.each_index do |y|
				data[y + 1][x + 1] = true if qr.dark?(x, y)
			end
		end
```

title: Can't generate black QR codes using `000000`
author: barnaclebarnes
body:
When trying to generate a black QR code as using the color `000000` you get a white QR code. If you use `#000000` you get a black QR code as expected. Below us example code and example output.

```
require "rqrcode"

qr = RQRCode::QRCode.new("https://kyan.com")
png = qr.as_png(color: "000000")
File.open("qr-white.png", "w") { |f| f.write png }

png = qr.as_png(color: "#000000")
File.open("qr-black.png", "w") { |f| f.write png }
```

## Black QR code:

![qr-black](https://github.com/whomwah/rqrcode/assets/10322/af600aa2-433c-4c5b-b7cc-cdf99115387e)

## White QR code:

![qr-white](https://github.com/whomwah/rqrcode/assets/10322/96c42bb1-7f16-4956-93b5-cbd143ca8336)

title: SVG formatting is done with style attribute instead of fill, causing strict content security policies to fail
author: Jonakemon
body:
Hi there, we're a long-time user of the RQRCode gem. Thanks for maintaining it!

# Problem with CSP
In the past months we've moved to more strict CSP (content security policy) directives. In particular, the style directive conflicts with the way RQRCode implements the formatting of svg. RQRCode uses a style attribute, which conflicts with a style directive that does prohibits the use of inline styling.

For example, with the following CSP header, the svg underneath causes a CSP issue:
`Content-Security-Policy: style-src 'self';`
```svg
<svg version="1.1" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xmlns:ev="http://www.w3.org/2001/xml-events" viewBox="0 0 390 390" shape-rendering="crispEdges"><path d="M0 0h7v7h ..." style="fill: #000" transform="translate(0,0) scale(6)"></path></svg>
```

If `style="fill: #000"` would have been replaced with `fill: "#000"`, the CSP issue would not be raised.

# Support for the fill attribute

There should be no compatibility issues with this change:
- According to [caniuse.com/svg](https://caniuse.com/svg), all browsers support the fill attribute for svg tags.
- According to [css-tricks.com](https://css-tricks.com/almanac/properties/f/fill/), all values that are legal for the CSS fill attribute, are also legal for the svg tag fill attribute. One exception is the `url(#pattern)` that the CSS fill supports, which the svg tag fill does not support. For the use case of this library, I estimate this is not an issue.

# The changes
I'll open up a PR with the changes, which you can review. It's just a small change.

title: Resulting image blank
author: dvodvo
body:
This is a question.

I have not found any reference to this effect, but in testing QR code generation with a high number of characters, I have noted that consistently, past a certain quantity of characters, the image generated is a stream of #FFFFFF hashes.

If I increase the size to the image, then a proper QR code is generated.

The question is thus:  when a blank image (formed by a stream of #FFFFFF characters) is generated, is this indicative that the library cannot produce a QRcode with complete data and associated error correction ?
